Alright, so this is probably the easiest step in the whole process in terms of tech tools. I mentioned in my previous video, you'll want to use either WordPress or some sort of landing page builder. And so I'm just going to show you one example of the application page and one example of the scheduling page. And so this first one is from our AI. This is in WordPress. You can see that there is a embedded application. This is using involve.me. I'll show you the back end of that in another video. But essentially, you can see this page is very simply designed. And if we edit this, you'll see that all this is is a simple embed code and almost any tool that's worth its kind of weight in terms of being an application builder is going to have this. And no matter what kind of building framework you're using on WordPress, it's typically typically going to accept HTML. And so that's all this is, I'll show you an involve.me where you pull this, but every tool usually has a ability to embed. And you can see all I've done is grab that snippet, pasted it into an HTML element on the page, a custom HTML element. And that's it. So all that does is we'll load that into the page. So if I go back to the page, there you have it. So quite simple. And then as an alternative, this is just another example of a application funnel. This one was built in Convertory. This is acuity, you can see that acuity is embedded into a page. So we've done a little bit to kind of add some additional instructions as well as show some testimonials of someone's having doubt at this stage. But if we look at the back end of Convertory, this works very much like most visual drag and drop landing page builders. This is quite simply a HTML element just like in WordPress. So in Convertory, there is various types of elements you can choose. HTML element is one of them. And if I edit that, just like with involve.me for the application, acuity allows you to get an iframe embed of the scheduler. And this allows you to put it on your own page rather than just directing people to the acuity page itself, which is perfectly fine to do that. But this is just if you want to pretty it up a little bit. And there might be other reasons in terms of tracking, you might do this. So you can see we're just embedding that code here. And that's what gives us this effect. And yeah, there's not much else to this. Bring your questions if anything comes up. But this should work pretty much the same way regardless of what landing page building tool you're using.
